executive_summary: Child D of the breakage-detector orchestrator (A+B+C complete; E+F remain). Builds a NET-NEW, uniquely-named active canary prober (scripts/breakage/active-breakage-canary.mjs + a PURE classifier lib lib/breakage/active-canary-probes.cjs) that actively probes the 4 breakage classes child C's PASSIVE detectors do NOT cover — RLS-regression, gate-pipeline-down, payment-webhook-fail, model-availability-cap — and on detection writes FAIL-LOUD to system_alerts via child-B recordSystemAlert using child-A's frozen taxonomy. Distinct from llm_canary_* and from run-canary-probe.mjs/CANARY_VENTURE_PROBE_V1. SAFETY-FIRST probes (read-only; never trigger a payment, never weaken RLS, never mutate prod): RLS-regression compares anon-vs-service row VISIBILITY on an anon-select-denied governance table (no write); gate-pipeline-down flags only activity-WITH-failure (never an idle fleet); payment-webhook-fail is substrate-absence-aware (NO payment webhook table exists yet — Stripe test-mode — so it SKIPS, forward-compatible, zero false-positives); model-availability-cap REUSES detectFromDb from the SOLO-OPERATOR-CONTINUITY llm-degradation-detector (never re-implements the rung evaluator). FROZEN CONTRACT honored: break_class in metadata.break_class; alert_type via toAlertType() stays in the legal CHECK set (NO widening); severity in {info,warning,critical}; NO system_alerts schema migration.

functional_requirements.title: RLS-regression probe (read-only, no mutation)
functional_requirements.description: Detect that anon Row-Level-Security is still enforced WITHOUT any write. Compare visibility: service client counts rows in an anon-SELECT-DENIED governance table T (verified at build time, e.g. sub_agent_execution_results / leo_handoff_executions); anon client counts rows in T. REGRESSION iff service sees N>0 rows but anon ALSO sees rows (anon can read RLS-protected data). If N===0 the probe is inconclusive (empty table) -> no alert (fail-open). On regression -> recordSystemAlert(breakClass:'RLS-regression'). PURE classifier classifyRlsProbe({serviceCount, anonCount, anonError}) is unit-tested; the anon/service reads are thin IO.
acceptance_criteria: NO write/insert/update/delete on any prod table by this probe
regression flagged only when anon can read rows a service-role client sees in an anon-denied table
empty-table / anon-error cases are inconclusive -> no false alert (fail-open)

functional_requirements.title: gate-pipeline-down probe (activity-with-failure, never idle)
functional_requirements.description: Detect the LEO gate pipeline being BROKEN, not merely idle. PURE classifier classifyGatePipelineProbe(recentExecutions, nowMs) over recent leo_handoff_executions / sd_phase_handoffs: flag gate-pipeline-down ONLY when there is recent handoff ACTIVITY in the window AND it is failing at/above a threshold (e.g. recent executions present AND >=X consecutive infra/gate errors, or a recent-accept rate of 0 despite recent attempts). An IDLE fleet (no recent attempts) is NORMAL -> no alert (the key false-positive guard). On detection -> recordSystemAlert(breakClass:'gate-pipeline-down').
acceptance_criteria: an idle window (no recent handoff attempts) NEVER fires (no idle false-positive)
fires only on recent activity that is failing (attempts present + accept-rate collapse / infra errors)
the classifier is pure + unit-tested for idle / healthy / broken

functional_requirements.title: payment-webhook-fail probe (substrate-absence-aware, forward-compatible)
functional_requirements.description: The payment-webhook substrate does NOT exist yet (no webhook_events/payment_*/stripe_* table; Stripe is test-mode/not-live). The probe first checks whether the webhook-health table exists; if ABSENT it SKIPS (logs 'payment-webhook substrate not present', emits NO alert -> zero false-positives) and is forward-compatible (activates when the table lands). When present (future), it reads the last-processed-at / error-count health signal and flags staleness/errors. PURE classifier classifyPaymentWebhookProbe({tablePresent, lastProcessedAtMs, errorCount, nowMs}).
acceptance_criteria: absent substrate -> SKIP, NO alert, clear log (honest: no payment substrate today)
present substrate (future) -> flags stale/erroring webhook processing
the classifier is pure + unit-tested for absent / healthy / stale

functional_requirements.title: model-availability-cap probe (reuse detectFromDb, don't duplicate)
functional_requirements.description: REUSE detectFromDb(supabase, nowMs) + RUNG + isDegradedSafeMode from scripts/continuity/llm-degradation-detector.mjs (SOLO-OPERATOR-CONTINUITY) — do NOT re-implement the rung evaluator. A degraded rung (SINGLE_SESSION/MODEL_FALLBACK/PAUSE_AND_SURFACE) -> recordSystemAlert(breakClass:'model-availability-cap') with severity mapped from the rung (PAUSE_AND_SURFACE->critical else warning, within {info,warning,critical}). NORMAL -> no alert. Honest note: the live llm_canary_state is dormant (reads NORMAL in prod today, per the detector's disclosed limitation) — the probe is correct + forward-active when the cloud-health feeder lands.
acceptance_criteria: imports + calls the existing detectFromDb (no duplicate rung logic)
degraded rung -> one model-availability-cap alert with a legal severity; NORMAL -> none
rung->severity mapping pinned

functional_requirements.title: The active canary CLI (cron/scriptable, fail-loud writes, --dry-run)
functional_requirements.description: scripts/breakage/active-breakage-canary.mjs (uniquely named; NOT llm_canary_*/run-canary-probe): resolves the anon + service clients, runs all 4 probes, and on any detected breakage writes FAIL-LOUD via recordSystemAlert (a write error surfaces / non-zero exit — per scope). Per-probe DETECTION is isolated (a probe that can't read its signal logs + continues so one blind probe doesn't suppress the others), but the alert WRITE is fail-loud. Supports --dry-run (classify + report, NO writes) for safe testing/cron-rehearsal. Prints a per-class summary. @wire-check reachable via a package.json scripts entry.
acceptance_criteria: runnable as a cron/CLI (node scripts/breakage/active-breakage-canary.mjs [--dry-run]) + a package.json script entry
writes are fail-loud (recordSystemAlert); per-probe detection is isolated
--dry-run performs NO writes and never triggers a payment / mutates prod

functional_requirements.title: Tests (pure classifiers + dry-run)
functional_requirements.description: tests/unit pins for each pure classifier: RLS enforced/regressed/inconclusive; gate-pipeline idle(no-fire)/healthy/broken; payment-webhook absent(skip)/healthy/stale; model-availability NORMAL/degraded->severity; + the frozen break_class->legal alert_type round-trip for the 4 classes; + a dry-run that performs no writes (injected fake clients).
acceptance_criteria: each probe classifier pinned across its states (esp. the no-false-positive cases: RLS inconclusive, gate idle, payment absent)
frozen-taxonomy round-trip pinned for the 4 classes (alert_type legal, no widening)
tests use injected fakes (no live DB, no writes)

technical_requirements.title: Consume the frozen contract
technical_requirements.description: Import BREAK_CLASSES/toAlertType (child A) + recordSystemAlert (child B) + detectFromDb (SOLO-OPERATOR-CONTINUITY) — never redefine the taxonomy, never hand-roll a system_alerts insert, never duplicate the rung evaluator. Net-new name distinct from llm_canary_*/run-canary-probe.mjs/CANARY_VENTURE_PROBE_V1.

technical_requirements.title: Safety: read-only, no side effects
technical_requirements.description: No probe writes/mutates a prod table, triggers a real payment, or weakens RLS. RLS probe is read-only (visibility comparison). All detection is read-only; the only writes are the alert rows themselves (fail-loud) and only on a detected breakage.

technical_requirements.title: Out of scope
technical_requirements.description: No system_alerts schema migration; no alert_type CHECK widening; no reuse/rename of llm_canary_*/run-canary-probe.mjs; no re-implementing child C's passive detectors; no chairman board (child E); no building the payment-webhook substrate (only forward-compatible detection).

test_scenarios.scenario: RLS classifier: enforced (anon 0 / service N) vs regressed (anon sees rows) vs inconclusive (N=0)
test_scenarios.expected: regression only when anon reads RLS-protected rows; else no alert

test_scenarios.scenario: gate-pipeline classifier: idle->no fire, healthy->no fire, activity+failing->fire

test_scenarios.expected: absent substrate emits nothing; degraded rung maps to a legal severity
injected fakes record zero inserts in --dry-run

acceptance_criteria: A net-new uniquely-named active canary probes RLS-regression / gate-pipeline-down / payment-webhook-fail / model-availability-cap and writes fail-loud to system_alerts via recordSystemAlert with the frozen taxonomy; cron/scriptable + --dry-run
Every probe is read-only and safe (no prod mutation, no payment trigger, no RLS weakening); the no-false-positive guards (RLS inconclusive, gate idle, payment absent) hold

No schema migration / no alert_type widening / no llm_canary or run-canary reuse; adversarial review of per-class false-pos/neg + probe safety + dedup + reachability clean; tests pin every classifier state

risks.risk: RLS probe mutates prod or weakens RLS while testing it
risks.mitigation: Read-only visibility comparison (anon vs service row counts on an anon-denied table) — NO write at all. TR-2 + TS-1 pin no-write.

risks.risk: gate-pipeline-down false-fires on an idle fleet
risks.mitigation: Classifier requires recent ACTIVITY + failure; idle -> NORMAL. TS-2 pins the idle case.

risks.risk: payment-webhook probe fabricates a signal with no substrate
risks.mitigation: Substrate-absence-aware: SKIP when no table; honest log; forward-compatible. TS-3 pins absent->skip.

risks.risk: Duplicating the llm degradation rung evaluator
risks.mitigation: Import + call detectFromDb; map rung->severity only. TS-3 pins the mapping; no rung logic re-implemented.

consumers: chairman breakage surface (child E) + catch-rate harness (child F) read the system_alerts rows
cron/CI runs the canary on a cadence
data_contracts: break_class in metadata.break_class; alert_type via toAlertType() legal; severity in {info,warning,critical}; dedup on (source_service, break_class) open-alert; NO schema change
runtime_config: --dry-run (no writes); per-probe source-table config; cron cadence via the npm script
observability_rollout: system_alerts rows visible to children E/F; per-class summary on stdout; unit pins assert classifiers + no-false-positive guards

system_architecture.summary: A PURE classifier lib (4 probe-result classifiers + taxonomy round-trip) + an ESM CLI that wires the anon/service clients + detectFromDb + recordSystemAlert, runs the probes (read-only detection, fail-loud writes), with a --dry-run.

components.name: lib/breakage/active-canary-probes.cjs
components.change: NEW: pure classifyRlsProbe / classifyGatePipelineProbe / classifyPaymentWebhookProbe / classifyModelAvailabilityProbe + rung->severity

components.name: scripts/breakage/active-breakage-canary.mjs
components.change: NEW: CLI — resolve clients, run 4 probes, fail-loud recordSystemAlert, --dry-run, package.json entry

components.name: tests/unit/active-breakage-canary.test.js
components.change: NEW: FR-D6 pins (every classifier state + round-trip + dry-run no-write)

components.name: package.json
components.change: MODIFY: add a breakage:canary script entry (reachability + cron)

implementation_approach.summary: Pure, unit-testable probe classifiers (with explicit no-false-positive guards) + a thin fail-loud CLI that reuses child A/B + the llm-degradation-detector; verify each probe is read-only/safe; adversarially review per-class correctness + safety before merge.
steps: build the CLI (read-only detection, fail-loud writes, --dry-run) + verify the anon-denied table for the RLS probe

smoke_test_steps.instruction: npx vitest run the new active-canary probe tests
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: each probe (RLS-regression, gate-pipeline-down, payment-webhook-fail, model-availability-cap) maps a DETECTED breakage to the correct frozen break_class + legal alert_type via recordSystemAlert; a HEALTHY probe emits nothing

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Run the active canary prober against seeded/injected per-class breakage (dry-run, no prod mutation)
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: a seeded breakage per class writes ONE fail-loud system_alerts row with metadata.break_class set; the prober NEVER triggers a real payment, mutates prod, or weakens RLS

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Confirm break-class encoding round-trips via metadata->>break_class and alert_type stays in the legal alert_type set
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: metadata->>break_class returns the frozen class; alert_type derived via toAlertType() is within the existing legal set (no CHECK widening)
